titleStmt.title: Letter from Rupert Stanley to his colleagues, 3 March 1920
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Rupert Stanley

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Call for contributions for the purpose of commissioning an oil portrait of Captain Francis Forth to hang in the library.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.persName: staff of the Municipal Technical Institute, Belfast

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Telephone No 1168 MAJOR RUPERT STANLEY
B.A., M.I.E.E., F.I.R.E.
PRINCIPAL. Municipal Techinical Institute
Belfast 3rd March, 1920. Dear Sir (or Madam), At a recent meeting of the permanent staff
of the Municipal Techincal Institute It was decided
to tale steps to perpetuate, in the Institute, the memory
of the late Principal - Capt. Fras. C. Forth. The Memorial is to take the form of a
portrait in Oils which will be hung in the Institute
Library. Several members of the Occasional Teaching
Staff have expressed a desire to take part in this
movement; I am therefore writing to say that all who wish
to do so may contribute to the Memorial Fund. Contributions will be received at the General
Offie of the Institute, or they may be addressed to the
Hon. Treasurer, Professor Earls, Municipal Technical
Istitute, Belfast. Yours faithfully,
Rupert Stanley
Principal.
